title: EuroTransit - Infrastructure (USE / Golden Signals)
description: Utilization, Saturation, Errors per node - the resource-level counterpart to the per-service RED dashboard. Built directly from this session's real incident: nodes silently hitting memory/CPU-credit exhaustion while every workload had zero declared resource requests, invisible to the scheduler.

panels.title: Utilization — CPU % by node
panels.description: USE: Utilization. How busy is each node's CPU?

panels.title: Utilization — Memory % by node
panels.description: USE: Utilization. This is the metric that mattered most this session - real memory usage vs allocatable.

panels.title: Utilization — Memory requests vs allocatable, by node
panels.description: The OTHER half of the picture: what the scheduler thinks is used (declared requests), which can diverge sharply from actual usage above if workloads don't declare resources.requests - exactly what caused this session's node instability.

targets.expr: 100 * sum by (node) (kube_pod_container_resource_requests{resource="memory"} * on(namespace,pod) group_left() (kube_pod_status_phase{phase="Running"} == 1)) / on(node) group_left() sum by (node) (kube_node_status_allocatable{resource="memory"})

panels.title: Saturation — CPU throttling (CFS throttled periods)
panels.description: USE: Saturation. Containers being throttled indicates real CPU contention, not just high utilization - directly relevant on burstable/credit-based VM SKUs.

panels.title: Saturation — Load average (5m) by node
panels.description: USE: Saturation. Work queued waiting for CPU.

panels.title: Saturation — Pods Pending (unschedulable)
panels.description: USE: Saturation. Directly counts what we hit repeatedly this session: pods that can't be scheduled anywhere.

panels.title: Errors — Node Ready condition
panels.description: USE: Errors. 1 = Ready, 0 = NotReady. This session's recurring node-flapping symptom, made visible directly instead of discovered via a failed kubectl command.

panels.title: Errors — Node MemoryPressure condition
panels.description: USE: Errors. Kubelet's own signal that the node considers itself memory-constrained.

panels.title: Errors — OOMKilled containers (last 1h)
panels.description: USE: Errors. Direct evidence of under-provisioned memory limits.

panels.title: Errors — Pod container restarts (last 10m), all namespaces
Errors — Pods currently unschedulable
panels.description: kube_pod_status_scheduled is a gauge (per-condition state), not a counter - this is a point-in-time count of pods stuck with PodScheduled=false right now, the exact 'Insufficient memory/cpu' state this session hit repeatedly. Not the same as Pending count above: a pod can be Pending for other reasons (e.g. image pull) without this condition being false.
